title: Dyspareunia documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines chronic pelvic pain as a symptom rather than a diagnosis and frames initial assessment around the woman’s concerns, function, and multifactorial context.
presenting_history.rationale: Applies only in chronic non-pregnancy pelvic pain.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines chronic pelvic pain as a symptom rather than a diagnosis and frames initial assessment around the woman’s concerns, function, and multifactorial context.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

exact_section.heading: 1. Purpose and Scope; 2. Background; 5. Initial Assessment

examination.text: Specifies pain pattern, menstrual, bladder, bowel, movement, posture, function and medication history plus abdominal and pelvic examination for focal tenderness or structural findings.
examination.rationale: Supports sensitive history and clinician-performed examination when explicitly documented.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies pain pattern, menstrual, bladder, bowel, movement, posture, function and medication history plus abdominal and pelvic examination for focal tenderness or structural findings.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected STI sampling when PID is suspected and transvaginal ultrasound for adnexal masses and selected chronic pelvic-pain pathology.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-selected testing in qualified contexts.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected STI sampling when PID is suspected and transvaginal ultrasound for adnexal masses and selected chronic pelvic-pain pathology.

source.title: The Initial Management of Chronic Pelvic Pain — Green-top Guideline No. 41

exact_section.heading: 6.1 Screening for Infection; 6.2 Transvaginal Scanning and MRI

curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
